prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(select_booking_option channel_a_booking_1)
(select_booking_option channel_b_booking_1)
(select_booking_option master_booking_1)
(select_booking_option master_booking_2)
(assign_channel_identifier_to_booking channel_a_booking_1 channel_identifier_1)
(assign_channel_identifier_to_booking channel_b_booking_1 channel_identifier_2)
(assign_channel_identifier_to_booking master_booking_1 channel_identifier_3)
(request_supplier_confirmation channel_a_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(commit_reservation_on_confirmation channel_a_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(allocate_fulfillment_item channel_a_booking_1 fulfillment_item_1)
(stage_channel_a_reference channel_a_booking_1 channel_a_reference_1 supplier_token_1)
(confirm_channel_a_booking_allocation channel_a_booking_1 channel_a_reference_1 fulfillment_item_1)
(release_fulfillment_item channel_a_booking_1 fulfillment_item_1)
(release_supplier_confirmation_token channel_a_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(request_supplier_confirmation channel_b_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(commit_reservation_on_confirmation channel_b_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(allocate_fulfillment_item channel_b_booking_1 fulfillment_item_1)
(stage_channel_b_reference channel_b_booking_1 channel_b_reference_1 supplier_token_1)
(confirm_channel_b_booking_allocation channel_b_booking_1 channel_b_reference_1 fulfillment_item_1)
(register_aligned_booking_reference_basic channel_a_booking_1 channel_b_booking_1 channel_a_reference_1 channel_b_reference_1 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(release_supplier_confirmation_token channel_b_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(request_supplier_confirmation channel_a_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(prepare_aligned_reference_for_payment aligned_booking_ref_1 channel_a_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(sync_finalization_to_channel_a_booking channel_a_booking_1 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(sync_finalization_to_channel_b_booking channel_b_booking_1 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(release_fulfillment_item channel_b_booking_1 fulfillment_item_1)
(release_supplier_confirmation_token channel_a_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(request_supplier_confirmation master_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(commit_reservation_on_confirmation master_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(link_payment_record_to_aligned_reference master_booking_1 payment_record_1 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(finalize_master_booking_financials master_booking_1 payment_record_1 aligned_booking_ref_1 supplier_token_1)
(assign_supplier_allocation_to_master_booking master_booking_1 supplier_allocation_1)
(allocate_fulfillment_item master_booking_1 fulfillment_item_1)
(assign_approver_to_master_booking master_booking_1 approver_role_1)
(assign_supplier_and_rate_to_master_booking master_booking_1 supplier_allocation_1 fulfillment_item_1 payment_record_1 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(obtain_approval_for_master_booking master_booking_1 approver_role_1 payment_record_1 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(initiate_master_booking_finalization master_booking_1)
(generate_alignment_proof_for_booking master_booking_1 sync_token_1 supplier_token_1)
(apply_alignment_to_master_booking master_booking_1 channel_identifier_3 sync_token_1)
(assign_channel_identifier_to_booking master_booking_2 channel_identifier_3)
(release_supplier_confirmation_token master_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(request_supplier_confirmation master_booking_2 supplier_token_1)
(commit_reservation_on_confirmation master_booking_2 supplier_token_1)
(link_payment_record_to_aligned_reference master_booking_2 payment_record_2 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(finalize_master_booking_financials master_booking_2 payment_record_2 aligned_booking_ref_1 supplier_token_1)
(release_supplier_allocation_from_master_booking master_booking_1 supplier_allocation_1)
(release_fulfillment_item master_booking_1 fulfillment_item_1)
(remove_approver_from_master_booking master_booking_1 approver_role_1)
(assign_supplier_allocation_to_master_booking master_booking_2 supplier_allocation_1)
(allocate_fulfillment_item master_booking_2 fulfillment_item_1)
(assign_approver_to_master_booking master_booking_2 approver_role_1)
(assign_supplier_and_rate_to_master_booking master_booking_2 supplier_allocation_1 fulfillment_item_1 payment_record_2 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(obtain_approval_for_master_booking master_booking_2 approver_role_1 payment_record_2 aligned_booking_ref_1)
(initiate_master_booking_finalization master_booking_2)
(generate_alignment_proof_for_booking master_booking_2 sync_token_1 supplier_token_1)
(apply_alignment_to_master_booking master_booking_2 channel_identifier_3 sync_token_1)
(release_supplier_confirmation_token master_booking_2 supplier_token_1)
(request_supplier_confirmation channel_a_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(generate_alignment_proof_for_booking channel_a_booking_1 sync_token_1 supplier_token_1)
(apply_alignment_to_channel_a_booking channel_a_booking_1 channel_identifier_1 sync_token_1)
(release_supplier_confirmation_token channel_a_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(request_supplier_confirmation channel_b_booking_1 supplier_token_1)
(generate_alignment_proof_for_booking channel_b_booking_1 sync_token_1 supplier_token_1)
(apply_alignment_to_channel_b_booking channel_b_booking_1 channel_identifier_2 sync_token_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a classical planning problem in PDDL for cross-channel booking reference alignment. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Examining planning problem  
- The task involves a classical planning problem in PDDL for cross-channel booking reference alignment.  
- The output must be an executable plan of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).

## Examining planning problem  
- The goal is to achieve record_aligned for channel_a_booking_1, channel_b_booking_1, master_booking_1, and master_booking_2.  
- Initial state shows both master bookings linked to the same channel bookings and aligned_booking_ref_1, with shared resources like payment records and policies.  

## Investigating alignment process  
- To get record_aligned, the process ends with apply_alignment_to_* actions, requiring sync_proof_bound from generate_alignment_proof_for_booking after record_finalized.  
- Record_finalized comes from finalize actions like finalize_master_booking or sync_finalization_to_channel_*, and generate requires booking_has_channel_identifier, booking_linked_sync_token, and a supplier_token.  

## Analyzing initial conditions  
- Only booking_policy_1 is available initially, but master_booking_2 is linked to booking_policy_2, which isn’t available, complicating the alignment.  
- Other available resources include approver_role_1, supplier_allocation_1, sync_token_1, and various customer profiles, rate options, and payments, starting unselected.

## Investigating alignment process  
- Multiple bookings share one supplier_token_1, which can be held, used, and released multiple times to manage resource constraints.  
- Alignment requires careful sequencing of actions like stage_channel_reference and confirm_allocation, reusing resources like fulfillment and supporting documents.  

## Exploring channel alignment paths  
- Channel bookings can align via staging with a token (stage_channel_reference, then confirm_allocation) or using supporting documents (attach_supporting_document, then process_confirmation).  
- Both paths ensure bookings are ready, with effects like setting staged, reserved, or allocation_confirmed states, and may release documents after processing.  

## Analyzing registration variants  
- Registering aligned bookings has variants like basic (both staged, allocation confirmed) or with_a_flag (one reserved, one staged, specific allocation states).  
- Registration requires both bookings ready, with preconditions varying by path, such as staging or reserving references.  

## Planning payment preparation  
- After registration, prepare for payment by ensuring channel_a is ready and holds the supplier token, setting ready_for_payment.  
- Finalizing financials involves actions like finalize_master_booking_financials, requiring committed status, linked payments, and token holding, with variants for rate assignments.

## Examining Channel ID Constraints  
- Only three channel identifiers (channel_identifier_1, 2, 3) are available for four bookings, creating a resource constraint.  
- Once assigned via assign_channel_identifier_to_booking, a channel ID is marked as unavailable and cannot be reused until released by apply_alignment_to_*.  

## Investigating ID Reuse Challenges  
- Reusing a channel ID requires aligning and finalizing a booking first, but alignment needs a channel ID to be assigned, creating a circular dependency.  
- Finalizing a booking involves stages like registering aligned channels and preparing for payment, all requiring a channel ID initially.  

## Exploring Master Booking Finalization  
- Master bookings can initiate finalization with approval obtained, independent of channel finalization, via obtain_approval_*.  
- Finalizing masters still links to channel states, requiring aligned, registered channels and readiness for payment, tied to channel ID availability.

## Investigating Master Finalization Process  
- Finalizing master1 involves linking payment, which requires aligned, registered channels and readiness for payment, all needing a channel ID.  
- The process can proceed without profile attachment, using obtain_approval_for_master_booking after financials and supplier allocation, effecting finalized status.  

## Sequencing Token Usage  
- Token sharing is necessary for committing channels and masters, requiring careful sequencing to handle one token across multiple entities.  
- After finalizing master1, the token can be reused for subsequent steps, like generating proof for alignment and releasing channel IDs.  

## Planning Execution Order  
- Channels must be processed first (select, assign IDs, commit) to enable registration and payment preparation for masters.  
- After channels are ready, align and register them, then link payments for masters, ensuring token availability for financial finalization.

## Processing Master1 Commitment  
- Master1, with channel ID3 assigned, requires token commitment, currently held by channel_a.  
- Release token from channel_a, commit master1, and link payment to aligned, marking it consumed and finalized for payment.  

## Handling Financial Finalization  
- Finalize master1 financials using committed status, linked payment, and held token, effecting financials finalized.  
- Token remains held post-finalization, enabling subsequent alignment steps.  

## Managing Supplier and Rate Assignment  
- Assign supplier allocation and fulfillment to master1, releasing from channel_b if necessary, ensuring no flags.  
- Assign approver and obtain approval for master1, proceeding without profile attachment, leading to finalization.  

## Generating Alignment Proof  
- Generate proof for master1 using finalized status, held token, and sync availability, binding proof and linking sync.  
- Proof generation makes sync unavailable, preparing for alignment application with channel identifiers.

## Releasing and Requesting Tokens  
- Release the supplier confirmation token from master2 to use for channel_a_booking_1, then request confirmation for channel_a.  
- Generate and apply alignment proof for channel_a_booking_1, making ID1 available and sync ready, using sync_token_1 and supplier_token_1.  

## Managing Channel Alignments  
- Repeat token release and request for channel_b_booking_1, ensuring alignment proof generation and application with channel_identifier_2.  
- Both channel_a and channel_b are now aligned, completing the process without needing supporting documents.  

## Verifying Process Completeness  
- Confirmed all prerequisites for basic registration, including staged references and allocation confirmation for both channels, with no linked documents required.  
- The process handled two masters (master1 and master2) with separate payments, ensuring no shared payment issues, and aligned bookings correctly.

## Finalizing Channel Syncs  
- Finalize sync for channel_a_booking_1 and channel_b_booking_1 using aligned_booking_ref_1, marking both channels as finalized.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
